question: Is there a Python SDK for Stellar, and does it support the new Stellar RPC as well as Horizon?

golden.answer:
Yes. Use the community-maintained PyPI package **`stellar-sdk`**, whose repository is **`StellarCN/py-stellar-base`**: `pip install --upgrade stellar-sdk`, then import from `stellar_sdk`. It is an **off-chain client SDK** for transaction/XDR work and provides clients for both **Horizon and Stellar RPC**; it is not the Rust on-chain contract SDK.

As of **2026-07-04**, PyPI showed stable **15.0.0** with **Python >=3.10,<4**. The current project README documented **PyPy 3.11**. Recheck PyPI and the repository before pinning because those values are release-sensitive. Keep the similarly named Python package separate from the deprecated unscoped npm `stellar-sdk`, whose current JavaScript successor is `@stellar/stellar-sdk`.
keyFacts: Names PyPI package stellar-sdk and repository StellarCN/py-stellar-base and labels them community-maintained.
States current Horizon and Stellar RPC client support and the off-chain client role.
Dates 15.0.0 to 2026-07-04 with Python >=3.10,<4 and project-documented PyPy 3.11.
Distinguishes the Python package from both Rust soroban-sdk and the deprecated unscoped npm package.
avoid: Do NOT call the Python SDK SDF-maintained, Horizon-only, or an on-chain Python contract SDK.
Do NOT install a package named py-stellar-base when the published package is stellar-sdk.
Do NOT freeze 14.1.1, PyPy 3.10, or any current version/runtime value without an as-of date.
Do NOT confuse PyPI stellar-sdk with the deprecated npm package of the same unscoped name.
golden.notes: GT-56 FRESHNESS CORRECTION 2026-07-11: PyPI stellar-sdk / StellarCN py-stellar-base is community-maintained and supports Horizon plus RPC. Stable 15.0.0 was released 2026-07-04 with Python >=3.10,<4; the project documents PyPy 3.11. Date and recheck versions.

corroboration.claim: The community-maintained Python package is stellar-sdk from StellarCN/py-stellar-base and supports both Horizon and Stellar RPC.

evidence.ref: B: current StellarCN/py-stellar-base README and source

verified.by: Golden-truth sweep import — The inherited package identity and Horizon/RPC capability were correct, but its pinned 14.1.1 and PyPy 3.10 claims had already drifted and the current official SDK catalog still omitted the RPC capability.
evidence: Solo scratchpad 575 GT-56 primary process 3394 and sealed blind process 3398 independently confirmed package/repository identity and Horizon+RPC support while correcting version and PyPy drift.

Sibling sweep checked q-tool-official-sdks-list, q-tool-which-sdk-comparison, q-ti-provision-wallet-per-user, and q-ti-sdk-package-rename; the Python package is kept distinct from the deprecated npm name.
The documentation omission was already filed as improvements/stellar-docs/sd-033-python-sdk-rpc-capability-omitted.md; no duplicate was created.
rootCause: Eval-side package/runtime freshness drift from a prior PyPI snapshot.
